zh.lifeSummary: Margaret Massey是Hamon FitzRichard de Massey和Eleanor de Horton的女兒，出生在英國藍卡爾利斯頓。她於1440年與Sir Thomas Hesketh在英國藍卡爾盧福德結婚。他們至少是9個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1456年在英國英格蘭藍卡爾盧福德。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Margaret Massey是Hamon FitzRichard de Massey和Eleanor de Horton的女儿，出生在英国藍卡爾利斯頓。她于1440年与Sir Thomas Hesketh在英国藍卡爾盧福德结婚。他们至少是9个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1456年在英国英格兰藍卡爾盧福德。

nameMeanings.name: Massey
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Massy, a pet form of Matthew . Compare Mace .
English (of Norman origin): habitational name from one or more of several places in Normandy, such as Macey (Manche), Massy (Seine-Maritime), Macé-sur-Orne (Orne), and La Ferté Macé (Orne).
English: variant of Marsay (see Marcey ).

variants.name: Mace
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Masse, probably a shortened form of Matthew (compare Massey and Massett ) or possibly of Thomas .
French and Breton (Macé): from the French personal name Macé, a vernacular form of Mathieu ‘Matthew’. Compare Masse 5 and Maze 3.

French (Macé): habitational name from Macé or La Ferté-Macé in Orne, the former so named from Gallo-Roman Macciacum (from the personal name Maccius + the locative suffix -acum), the latter from the personal name Macé (see 2 above).

variants.name: Massie
meanings: Scottish (Aberdeen): variant of   Massey  .

variants.name: Marsee
meanings: English: altered form of   Massey  .

variants.name: Massee
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Germain.
Possibly an altered form of English   Massey  .
French (Massée): from the feminine form of Massé (see Masse ). This surname is rare in France.

variants.name: Massa
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Salvatore, Angelo, Sal, Antonio, Silvio, Annamarie, Biagio, Caesar, Carmela, Dante, Elisabetta, Ennio.

Italian: habitational name from any of various places called Massa, for example Massa Lubrense or Massa di Somma, both in Naples province, or Masse d'Albe in Aquila, all named from medieval Latin massa ‘farm, estate’ (from Latin massa ‘mass, lump, pile’, Greek maza). This surname is also found in southern France (see Massey 5).

variants.name: Matthew
meanings: English and Scottish: from the Middle English personal name Ma(t)thew, a vernacular form of the Greek New Testament name Matthias, Matthaios, which is ultimately from the Hebrew personal name Matityahu ‘gift of God’. This was taken into Latin as Mat(t)hias and Matthaeus respectively, the former being used for the twelfth apostle (who replaced Judas Iscariot) and the latter for the author of the first Gospel. In many European languages this distinction is reflected in different name forms. The commonest vernacular forms of the personal name, including English Matthew, Old French Matheu, Spanish Mateo, Italian Matteo, Portuguese Mateus, Catalan and Occitan Mateu are generally derived from the form Matthaeus. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed cognates from other languages, including German Matthias , Greek Mathias and Mattheos, Assyrian/Chaldean Matti , Czech Matěj, Slovak, Croatian, and Slovenian Matej . The name Matthew is also found among Christians in southern India (compare Mathai ), but since South Indians traditionally do not have hereditary surnames, the southern Indian name was in most cases registered as such only after immigration of its bearers to the US. Compare Mathew , see also Matthews .

variants.name: Macey
meanings: English: variant of   Massey  .
Americanized form of Slovak and Rusyn (from Slovakia) Macej or Mačej and Polish Maciej , derivatives of the Latin personal name Matthaeus (see Matthew ).

variants.name: Masey
meanings: English: variant of   Massey  .

variants.name: Macy
meanings: English: variant of   Massey  .

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: Margaret Massey was born in Rixton, Lancashire, England as the daughter of Hamon FitzRichard de Massey and Eleanor de Horton. She married Sir Thomas Hesketh in 1440, in Rufford, Lancashire, England. They were the parents of at least 9 sons and 1 daughter. She died in 1456, in Rufford, Lancashire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Margaret Massey est née à Rixton, Lancashire, Angleterre, fille de Hamon FitzRichard de Massey et Eleanor de Horton. Elle a épousé Sir Thomas Hesketh en 1440, à Rufford, Lancashire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 9 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée en 1456, à Rufford, Lancashire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Margaret Massey wurde in Rixton, Lancashire, England, als Tochter von Hamon FitzRichard de Massey und Eleanor de Horton geboren. Sie hat 1440 in Rufford, Lancashire, England Sir Thomas Hesketh geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 9 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Sie starb 1456 in Rufford, Lancashire, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Margaret Massey è nata il nel Rixton, Lancashire, England come figlia di Hamon FitzRichard de Massey e Eleanor de Horton. Lei sposato Sir Thomas Hesketh nel 1440, nel Rufford, Lancashire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 9 figli e 1 figlia. Morì nel 1456, nel Rufford, Lancashire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Margaret MasseyはイギリスランカシュアイアリクストンでHamon FitzRichard de MasseyとEleanor de Hortonの娘として生まれた。1440年にイギリスランカシュアイアルフォードでSir Thomas Heskethと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも9人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1456年にイギリスイギリスランカシュアイアルフォードで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret Massey(은)는 영국 랭카실르 리스턴에서 Hamon FitzRichard de Massey(와)과 Eleanor de Horton의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 1440년 영국 랭카실르 루퍼드에서 Sir Thomas Hesketh(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 아홉 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1456년 영국 잉글랜드 랭카실르 루퍼드에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Margaret Massey nasceu em Rixton, Lancashire, Inglaterra filha de Hamon FitzRichard de Massey e Eleanor de Horton. Ela casou-se com Sir Thomas Hesketh em 1440, em Rufford, Lancashire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 9 filhos e 1 filha. Ela faleceu em 1456, em Rufford, Lancashire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Margaret Massey родилась в местности рикстон, ланкашир, Англия. Её родителей звали Hamon FitzRichard de Massey и Eleanor de Horton. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Thomas Hesketh в 1440 г. в местности руффорд, ланкашир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 9 сыновей и 1 дочь. Она умерла в 1456 г. в местности руффорд, ланкашир, Англия, Великобритания.
